name: Public Route Eligibility Criteria v1
sitemap_eligibility_boundary: A route is not sitemap-eligible unless it exists, is public by governance, has exactly one canonical purpose, has no blocked capability implication, has sufficient substance, has approved metadata, has source/claim policy compliance where needed, has route registry approval, has public link governance, and has passed route readiness validation.
navigation_eligibility_boundary: A route is not navigation-eligible unless it has passed public route eligibility validation, route creation governance, public route readiness validation, has a safe public purpose, does not imply engine/classifier/upload/scoring/API behavior unless separately governed, and preserves Hoax.ai evidence posture boundary.
public_workbench_boundary: A public workbench route is not eligible by default. It requires separate public workbench governance, engine/classifier non-authorization review, upload/scoring prohibition or governance, public safety audit, privacy review, accessibility review, source/claim review, route readiness validation, sitemap governance, navigation governance, and deployment governance.
internal_prototype_boundary: The internal static prototype remains internal, static, non-public, non-operational, not route-registered, not sitemap-listed, not publicly linked, not a route candidate by default, not a public workbench, and not a launch surface. Public route eligibility governance must not expose, route, index, or link the internal prototype.
